author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

On the words of the Gospel, Luke xiii. 21 and 23, where the kingdom of God is said to be ‘like unto leaven, which a woman took and hid in three measures of meal;’ and of that which is written in the same chapter, ‘Lord, are they few that are saved?’

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Luke xiii. 21 and 23, where the kingdom of God is said to be ‘like unto leaven, which a woman took and hid in three measures of meal;’ and of that which is written in the same chapter, ‘Lord, are they few that are saved?’

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The three measures of meal” of which the Lord spake, is the human race.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Recollect the deluge; three only remained, from whom the rest were to be re-peopled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Noe had three sons, by them was repaired the human race.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That holy “woman who hid the leaven,” is Wisdom.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, the whole world crieth out in the Church of God, “I know that the Lord is great.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet doubtless there are but few who are saved.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye remember a question which was lately set before us out of the Gospel, “Lord,” it was said, “are there few that be saved?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What said the Lord to this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He did not say, “Not few, but many are they who are saved.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He did not say this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what said He, when He had heard, “Are there few that be saved?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Strive to enter by the strait gate.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When thou hearest then, “Are there few that be saved?” the Lord confirmed what He heard.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Through the “strait gate” but “few” can “enter.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In another place He saith Himself, “Strait and narrow is the way which leadeth unto life, and few there be that go thereby: but broad and spacious is the way that leadeth to destruction, and many there be which walk thereby.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why rejoice we in great numbers?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give ear to me, ye “few.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I know that ye are “many,” who hear me, yet but “few” of you hear to obey.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I see the floor, I look for the corn.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And hardly is the corn seen, when the floor is being threshed; but the time is coming, that it shall be winnowed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But few then are saved in comparison of the many that shall perish.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For these same “few” will constitute in themselves a great mass.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the Winnower shall come with His fan in His Hand, “He will cleanse His floor, and lay up the wheat into the garner; but the chaff He will burn with unquenchable fire.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let not the chaff scoff at the wheat; in this He speaketh truth, and deceiveth no one.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be ye then in yourselves among many a many, few though ye be in comparison of a certain many.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So large a mass is to come out of this floor, as to fill the garner of heaven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Lord Christ would not contradict Himself, who hath said, “Many there are who enter in by the narrow gate, many who go to ruin through the wide gate;” contradict Himself, who hath in another place said, “Many shall come from the East and West.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Many” then are the “few;” both “few” and “many.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Are the “few” one sort, and the “many” another?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No. But the “few” are themselves the “many;” “few” in comparison of the lost, “many in the society of the Angels.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hearken, dearly Beloved.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Apocalypse hath this written; “After this I beheld of all languages, and nations, and tribes, a great multitude, which no man can number, coming with white robes and palms.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is the mass of the saints.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: With how much clearer voice will the floor say, when it has been fanned, separated from the crowd of ungodly, and evil, and false Christians, when those who “press” and do not “touch” (for a certain woman in the Gospel “touched,” the crowd “pressed” Christ), shall have been severed unto everlasting fire; when all they then, who are to be damned shall have been separated off, with how great assurance will the purified mass, standing at the Right Hand, fearing now for itself the admixture of no evil men, nor the loss of any of the good, now about to reign with Christ, say, “I know that the Lord is great”!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then, my Brethren (I am speaking to the corn), if they acknowledge what I say, predestined unto life eternal, let them speak by their works, not by their voices.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am constrained to speak to you, what I ought not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I ought to find in you matter of praise, not to seek subjects for admonition.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet see I will say but a few words, I will not dwell upon it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Acknowledge the duty of hospitality, thereby some have attained unto God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou takest in some stranger, whose companion in the way thou thyself also art; for strangers are we all.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is a Christian who, even in his own house and in his own country, acknowledges himself to be a stranger.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For our country is above, there we shall not be strangers.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For every one here below, even in his own house, is a stranger.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If he be not a stranger, let him not pass on from hence.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If pass on he must, he is a stranger.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him not deceive himself, a stranger he is; whether he will or not, he is a stranger.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And he leaves that house to his children, one stranger to other strangers.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou wert at an inn, wouldest thou not depart when another comes?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The same thou doest even in thine own house.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thy father left a place to thee, thou wilt some day leave it to thy children.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Neither dost thou abide here, as one who is to abide always, nor to those who are so to abide, wilt thou leave it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If we are all passing away, let us do something which cannot pass away, that when we shall have passed away, and have come thither whence we may not pass away, we may find our good works there.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ is the keeper, why dost thou fear lest thou shouldest lose what thou spendest on the poor?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let us turn to the Lord,” etc.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And after the Sermon.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I suggest to you, Beloved, what ye know already.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To-morrow breaks the anniversary day of the venerable lord Aurelius’ ordination; he asks and admonishes you, dear Brethren, by my humble ministry, that ye would be so good as to meet together with all devotion at the basilica of Faustus.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thanks be to God.
